title: Amendment to Executive Order 13603 Regarding Delegations Under the Defense Production Act
summary: This executive order modifies Executive Order 13603 from March 16, 2012, which established procedures for national defense resources preparedness. The order adjusts certain delegations of presidential authorities that are granted under the Defense Production Act. The Defense Production Act provides the President with various powers related to industrial mobilization and resource allocation during national emergencies or defense-related situations. This order revises how specific authorities under that act are delegated among executive branch officials and agencies.

title: Executive Order on Advertising Standards for Products Labeled as Made in America
summary: This executive order establishes policy regarding the advertising and labeling of products marketed as made in America. The order asserts that consumers have a right to clear, accurate, and substantiated information about the manufacturing origin of products claiming to be made in the United States. The order directs implementation of measures intended to protect consumers against misleading claims regarding domestic manufacturing and to establish standards for truthfulness in advertising related to the Made in America designation.

title: Executive Order on Reducing Housing Construction Regulations
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to identify and remove regulatory requirements that the administration views as obstacles to new home construction and renovation. The order is based on the premise that reducing regulatory requirements will increase housing inventory and improve affordability. The order authorizes the President to direct executive branch agencies to streamline or eliminate specific regulations related to residential construction and renovation projects.

title: Executive Order on Mortgage Credit Access and Lending Standards
summary: This executive order directs federal agencies to establish policies and regulations aimed at expanding access to mortgage credit for American homebuyers. The order emphasizes ensuring that mortgage lending is based on individual creditworthiness and establishes a framework for federal involvement in mortgage lending practices. The order addresses the stated objective that all Americans seeking home purchases should have access to mortgages from reliable lenders with rates determined by their credit profiles. The executive order likely includes provisions for federal agencies to review existing lending standards, establish guidelines for mortgage origination, and potentially modify regulations affecting mortgage market participation and availability.

title: Presidential Proclamation on Women's History Month 2026
summary: A presidential proclamation issued in March 2026 acknowledging and recognizing the contributions of American women throughout the nation's history and in contemporary times. The proclamation commemorates women who have contributed to various sectors including family life, cultural development, defense, industry, and national advancement over a 250-year period. The proclamation marks the annual observance of Women's History Month.
